name: claude-bypass-permissions-unaffected
doc: Guard proof: handle-pre.ts only sets neverApproval when `id === "codex"` — Claude Code's OWN bypassPermissions mode (a real, documented `--permission-mode bypassPermissions` value) must NEVER be mistaken for Codex's approval_policy=never, even though both harnesses emit the identical `permission_mode:"bypassPermissions"` string. A `git commit` on claude-code with that field set still resolves to the ordinary interactive `permissionDecision:"ask"` — byte-identical to scenario 11, unaffected by this whole feature.

steps.doc: Same payload shape as scenario 28 step 1 (permission_mode bypassPermissions + git commit) but spawned as `hook claude-code core` instead of `hook codex core` — must resolve to the normal interactive ask, not the codex-only auto-approve warn.
